story.iWant: to implement dual-mode architecture (Original + Optimized) với feature flags
story.soThat: I can switch between modes và rollback anytime nếu có issues

task.title: Create OptimizationMode enum và OptimizationConfig class

subtasks.subtask: Create `OptimizationMode` enum với values: ORIGINAL, OPTIMIZED, AUTO
Make mode configurable via environment variable (e.g., `OPTIMIZATION_MODE`)
Add feature flags for individual optimizations (if needed)
Add conditional logic to switch between `_build_original_prompt()` và `_build_optimized_prompt()`
Ensure backward compatibility (default to ORIGINAL mode)
Test mode switching với different configurations
Integration test với actual LLM calls
Extract current prompt building logic từ `build_system_prompt()`
Ensure no changes to original logic (100% identical behavior)
Test original prompt generation matches current implementation
Integration test prompt structure matches current

Apply quality-preserving optimizations from Stories 1.1, 1.2, 1.3: - Restructure prompt với static content first (Story 1.1) - Enable LiteLLM Redis caching (Story 1.2) - Add Anthropic cache_control directives (Story 1.3)

task.title: Configure feature flags via environment variables

subtasks.subtask: Add optional feature flags for individual optimizations (if needed)
Test configuration loading từ environment variables
Test switching from ORIGINAL to OPTIMIZED mode
Test switching from OPTIMIZED back to ORIGINAL mode (rollback)

acceptanceCriteria.ac: `OptimizationConfig` class được implemented với feature flags
Feature flags được configurable via environment variables
Mode switching được tested và documented
Rollback mechanism được verified (switch to ORIGINAL mode anytime)

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Epic Breakdown: LLM Optimization
doc.snippet: Story 1.4 defines requirements for implementing dual-mode architecture. Enables all Phase 1 optimizations. Effort: 2 hours. Prerequisites: Stories 1.1, 1.2, 1.3.

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Master Optimization Plan v1.1
doc.snippet: Dual-mode architecture: Original mode (baseline) + Optimized mode (with caching). Feature flags allow switching and rollback. Mode selection via environment variable OPTIMIZATION_MODE.

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Master Optimization Plan v1.1
doc.snippet: PromptManager.build_system_prompt() switches between _build_original_prompt() và _build_optimized_prompt() based on OptimizationConfig.OPTIMIZATION_MODE. Original mode preserves current implementation exactly.

file.reason: Primary location to add OptimizationConfig class với OptimizationMode enum. Configuration class already exists và loads environment variables.
Primary method to modify for dual-mode support. Need to add conditional logic to switch between _build_original_prompt() và _build_optimized_prompt() based on OptimizationConfig.OPTIMIZATION_MODE.
New method to create. Extract current prompt building logic từ build_system_prompt() và move here. Preserve exact implementation (no changes).
New method to create. Apply quality-preserving optimizations from Stories 1.1, 1.2, 1.3. Restructure prompt với static content first, enable caching, add cache_control directives.

constraint.description: Zero quality impact required. Original mode must preserve current implementation exactly (100% identical behavior). Optimized mode must maintain 100% quality.
Dual-mode architecture: Original mode (baseline) + Optimized mode (with caching). Feature flags allow switching and rollback. Gradual rollout: 5% → 25% → 50% → 100% traffic.
Must not break existing LLM calls. Default to ORIGINAL mode. Changes should be transparent to callers. Test with existing agent configurations.
Mode switching must be configurable via environment variable OPTIMIZATION_MODE. Rollback mechanism must work correctly (switch to ORIGINAL mode anytime).
_build_original_prompt() must preserve current implementation exactly (no changes). Extract existing logic without modification.
_build_optimized_prompt() must integrate optimizations from Stories 1.1, 1.2, 1.3. Ensure all optimizations work together correctly.

interface.name: PromptManager.build_system_prompt()
interface.signature: async def build_system_prompt(...) -> dict: if OptimizationConfig.OPTIMIZATION_MODE == OptimizationMode.ORIGINAL: return await _build_original_prompt(...) elif OptimizationConfig.OPTIMIZATION_MODE == OptimizationMode.OPTIMIZED: return await _build_optimized_prompt(...)

interface.name: PromptManager._build_original_prompt()
interface.signature: async def _build_original_prompt(...) -> dict: # Current implementation (no changes)

interface.name: PromptManager._build_optimized_prompt()
interface.signature: async def _build_optimized_prompt(...) -> dict: # Apply optimizations from Stories 1.1, 1.2, 1.3

tests.standards: Testing standards include: Quality validation (100% identical behavior for ORIGINAL mode, 100% quality for OPTIMIZED mode), Performance testing (cost savings với OPTIMIZED mode, quality metrics), Integration testing (existing LLM calls, different models, agent configurations, mode switching, rollback mechanism). Use A/B testing framework for comparing original vs optimized modes.

test.idea: Unit test: Verify OptimizationMode enum và OptimizationConfig class creation
Integration test: Test configuration loading từ environment variables
Unit test: Verify mode switching logic trong build_system_prompt()
Integration test: Test mode switching với actual LLM calls
Unit test: Verify _build_original_prompt() generates identical prompts to current implementation
Integration test: Test original prompt structure matches current implementation exactly
Unit test: Verify _build_optimized_prompt() applies optimizations correctly
Integration test: Test optimized prompt structure và caching behavior
Unit test: Verify environment variable parsing và configuration loading
Integration test: Test configuration loading từ environment variables
Integration test: Test switching from ORIGINAL to OPTIMIZED mode
Integration test: Test rollback mechanism (switch from OPTIMIZED back to ORIGINAL)
A/B test: Run same queries with ORIGINAL and OPTIMIZED modes, compare quality metrics
